data.title: Afya Limited Announces Dividend Distribution of R$307.4 Million
data.description:
BELO HORIZONTE, Brazil--(BUSINESS WIRE)--March 12, 2026--

Afya Limited (; B3: A2FY34) ("Afya" or the "Company"), the leading medical education group and medical practice solutions provider in Brazil, announced that its board of directors (the "Board") approved its second distribution of a cash dividend.

On March 12, 2026, the Company's Board of Directors approved dividend distribution in the amount of R$307.4 million, representing 40% of the Company's consolidated net income for the year ended December 31, 2025 and a dividend per share of R$3.446838, payable in U.S. dollars on April 6, 2026, to the shareholders on record as of the close of business on March 25, 2025. The payment will be made at the exchange rate (PTAX) to be published by the Brazilian Central Bank on March 13, 2026.

"This dividend declaration reflects our disciplined approach to capital allocation and the strength of our capital structure," said Luis Blanco, Afya's CFO. "We also continue to execute our share repurchase program, with approximately 60% of the current authorized buyback program remaining available for execution until December 2026. Combined with dividends, we expect to distribute 50% of the Company's consolidated net income for the year ended 2025, reinforcing our commitment to shareholder returns. We remain confident in our long-term growth and value creation strategy and maintaining our growth guidance".

About Afya Limited (, B3: A2FY34)

Afya is a leading medical education group in Brazil based on the number of medical school seats, delivering an end-to-end physician-centric ecosystem that serves and empowers students and physicians to transform their ambitions into rewarding lifelong experiences from the moment they join us as medical students through their medical residency preparation, graduation program, continuing medical education activities and offering medical practice solutions to help doctors enhance their healthcare services through their whole career.

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260312969198/en/

CONTACT: Investor Relations Contact:

Afya Limited

ir@afya.com.br

data.title: Trimble CEO Robert G. Painter Sells 7,500 Shares
data.description:
Trimble President & CEO, Robert G. Painter, sold 7,500 shares of common stock on March 10, 2026, at a price of $70.45 per share, totaling $528,375. Following the transaction, Painter directly owns 19,397 shares and indirectly owns 210,846 shares through the Painter Family Irrevocable Trust. The sale was conducted under a rule 10b5-1 sale plan established on February 20, 2025.

Reporter NamePainter Robert G
RelationshipPresident & CEO
TypeSell
Amount$528,375
SEC FilingForm 4

data.title: Newlox Gold Ventures Corp.
data.description:
(TheNewswire)

    March 12, 2026 – TheNewswire -Vancouver, British Columbia – Newlox GoldVentures Corp. (“Newlox” or the “Company”) is pleased toannounce significant additions to its leadership team. These strategicappointments are intended to further enhance the Company'sgovernance, technical proficiency, and operational effectiveness as itpursues its growth objectives in sustainable gold production.

    Frederick Kozak, P.Eng.

    Mr. Kozak has been appointed to the Board of Directorsas an independent director. Mr. Kozak is a Professional Engineer andglobally recognized expert in critical minerals and resource sectorequities.

    With a distinguished career as a top-rankedinternational analyst at leading firms including Canaccord Genuity(Vice President and Senior Oil & Gas Analyst) and HaywoodSecurities, he earned consistent accolades for stock-pickingexcellence in Canada.

    Transitioning to executive roles, Mr. Kozak served asPresident of Appia Rare Earths & Uranium Corp., President &CEO of Auxico Resources Canada Inc., and Founder, President & CEOof Environmental Minerals Ltd., a company dedicated to ethicallysourced critical minerals and rare earth elements. He also co-foundeda public Colombian oil and gas producer. His deep industry knowledge,analytical rigor, and leadership in ethical resource development willprovide valuable strategic guidance to Newlox’s Board.

    Dr. Marcello Veiga

    Dr. Veiga, a world-renowned authority on sustainablemining and mercury reduction, has been appointed the Company’s LeadTechnical Advisor. As Professor Emeritus at the University of BritishColumbia’s Norman B. Keevil Institute of Mining Engineering (since1997), Dr. Veiga has over 25 years of experience as a metallurgicalengineer and environmental geochemist, with projects spanning Brazil,Canada, the USA, Venezuela, Chile, and Peru.

    From 2002 to 2008, he served as Chief Technical Advisorfor the UNIDO Global Mercury Project (GEF/UNDP/UNIDO), leading globalefforts to assess and mitigate mercury pollution in artisanal goldmining while developing innovative, low-emission recovery techniquesusing locally fabricated equipment.  In his expanded role as LeadTechnical Advisor, he will continue to drive the Company’s“Coexistence Model” for responsible partnerships with artisanalminers, advancing cleaner technologies and environmentalremediation.

    Wilmer Antonio Ñiquen Parra,P.Eng.

    Mr. Parra been appointed Head of Operations in theField which entails overseeing on-site operations for current andfuture projects. With more than 24 years as a metallurgical engineer,Mr. Ñiquen Parra has successfully built, optimized, and operatednumerous precious metals facilities across Peru, Ecuador, Colombia,and Brazil. His technical expertise encompasses gravimetric oreconcentration, flotation, chemical leaching, carbon elution,Merrill-Crowe recovery, smelting and refining, as well as wastewaterand tailings management. Recognized by the United Nations IndustrialDevelopment Organization (UNIDO) as an International Expert in MineralBeneficiation for his collaborative work in Colombia and Peru(supported by the UN and US State Department), Mr. Ñiquen Parra  inthis enhanced field-focused leadership position, will directly overseeoperational execution, plant optimizations, and expansion projects,ensuring high standards of efficiency, safety, and environmentalresponsibility.

    These appointments demonstrate Newlox Gold’sdedication to strengthening its team with highly qualifiedprofessionals in sustainability, operations, and governance. Theenhanced leadership structure positions the Company to expediteproject execution, deliver increased value for shareholders, andadvance its mission of responsible mineral production andenvironmental remediation.

    About Newlox GoldVentures Corp.

    Newlox GoldVentures Corp. is an emerging ore processor of precious metals with afocus on the recovery of gold and silver from artisanal andsmall-scale mining operations across Latin America. The Corporationleverages technology to recover precious metals while remediatinghistorical mine waste and contributing to local economicdevelopment.

    For further details, please contact:  

    info@newloxgold.com

    647.848.5843

    Neither Canadian Securities Exchangenor its Regulation Services Provider (as that term is defined in thepolicies of the Canadian Securities Exchange) accept responsibilityfor the adequacy or accuracy of this release).

    Forward Looking Statements

    This news release contains“forward-looking statements” within the meaning of the UnitedStates Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995 and applicableCanadian securities legislation. Generally, these forward-lookingstatements can be identified by the use of forward-looking terminologysuch as “plans”, “anticipated”, “expects” or “does notexpect”, “is expected”, “budget”, “scheduled”,“estimates”, “forecasts”, “intends”, “anticipates” or“does not anticipate”, or “believes”, or variations of suchwords and phrases or state that certain actions, events or results“may”, “could”, “would”, “might” or “will betaken”, “occur” or “be achieved”. Newlox is subject tosignificant risks and uncertainties which may cause the actualresults, performance or achievements to be materially different fromany future results, performance or achievements expressed or impliedby the forward-looking statements contained in this release. Newloxcannot assure investors that actual results will be consistent withthese forward-looking statements and Newlox assumes no obligation toupdate or revise the forward-looking statements contained in thisrelease to reflect actual events or new circumstances.

data.title: Zumiez Q4 revenue, EPS jumps on strong full-price selling
data.description:
Overview

  • US specialty apparel retailer's Q4 revenue rose 4.4% yr/yr, EPS up 48.7%

  • Company attributes results to strong full-price selling and margin gains in North America and Europe

  • Zumiez board approved new $40 mln stock repurchase on March 11

Outlook

  • Zumiez projects fiscal Q1 net sales between $189 mln and $193 mln

  • Company expects Q1 EPS between a loss of $0.77 and a loss of $0.87

  • Zumiez plans to open 5 new stores and close 25 stores in fiscal 2026

Result Drivers

  • FULL-PRICE SELLING - Co said strong full-price selling in North America fueled comparable sales growth and gross margin expansion

  • EUROPEAN PRODUCT MARGIN - Co said focus on assortment and full-price selling in Europe led to 250 basis points of improvement in product margin yr/yr

  • MERCHANDISE AND EXPENSE MANAGEMENT - Co cited success of recent merchandise assortments and expense management as supporting Q4 results

data.title: Tianci International 10-Q: $7.70M Revenue; $(0.03) EPS
data.description:
Tianci International reported total operating revenues of $7.70 million for the six months ended Jan. 31, 2026, a 52% increase year-over-year driven by mineral sales and expanded logistics activity. The company recorded a net loss attributable to Tianci International of $0.686 million, or $(0.03) per basic and diluted share, reflecting higher general and administrative costs associated with public-company operations. Gross profit for the period was $0.475 million with a gross margin of approximately 6.16%.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Operating Revenues: $7.70 million (six months ended Jan. 31, 2026), up 52% vs prior year six months.
  • Gross profit: $0.475 million (gross margin ~6.16%).
  • (Loss) from operations: $(0.686) million, worsened vs prior year due to higher G&A from public company costs.
  • Net (loss): $(0.686) million for the six months ended Jan. 31, 2026 (includes non-controlling interest).
  • (Loss) per common share attributable to Tianci International: $(0.03) basic and diluted (six months ended Jan. 31, 2026).

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Growth: Total revenue rose 52% year-over-year to $7.70 million for the six-month period, driven by new mineral trading activities and logistics expansion.
  • Channel Shift: Launched global mineral trading (chrome and manganese), which contributed approximately 24% of six-month revenue.
  • Core Business Momentum: Logistics revenue increased 19% year-over-year, with growth across Asia‑Pacific and African routes.
  • Operational Milestone: Completed a $7 million public offering and Nasdaq listing on April 11, 2025, supporting the scaling of public-company operations.
  • Cost & Margin Dynamics: Logistics price competition pressured gross margins, while mineral trading revenues helped partially offset margin declines.

Original SEC Filing: Tianci International, Inc. [ CIIT ] - 10-Q - Mar. 12, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Limoneira 10-Q: Revenue $18.2M, EPS $(0.53) on Q1 net loss
data.description:
Limoneira reported first-quarter net revenues of $18.2 million and a basic net loss per common share of $(0.53), as the company recorded an operating loss of $(10.6) million driven primarily by lower agribusiness/lemon revenues. Net loss for the quarter was $(9.3) million, which included an income tax benefit of $2.7 million. Adjusted EBITDA was a $(7.7) million negative, reflecting adjustments for stock-based compensation, disposals and foreign exchange losses.

Financial Highlights

  • Total net revenues: $18.2 million, down 47% year-over-year (from $34.3 million), primarily due to lower agribusiness/lemon revenues.
  • Operating loss: $(10.6) million (the report uses the term "Operating loss" rather than operating income).
  • Net loss: $(9.3) million (includes an income tax benefit of $2.7 million).
  • Net loss applicable to common stock / Basic net loss per common share: $(9.55) million; basic net loss per common share $ (0.53).
  • EBITDA / Adjusted EBITDA (supplemental): EBITDA $(9.2) million; Adjusted EBITDA $(7.7) million (adjusted for stock compensation, disposals, and foreign exchange losses).

Business Highlights

  • Channel shift to Sunkist: Entered a Commercial Packinghouse License with Sunkist (effective November 1, 2025), transferring lemon sales, marketing and brokerage activities to Sunkist and shifting sales timing for the lemon business.
  • Revenue timing shift: Under the Sunkist arrangement, lemon volumes and fresh carton sales are expected to move from Q1–Q2 into Q3–Q4, lowering near‑term (Q1) revenues and creating expectations for later-season revenue recognition.
  • Packinghouse utilization & mix: Continued use of the company’s packinghouses while packing significant third‑party fruit—78% of Q1 cartons were third‑party—to optimize fixed costs and throughput.
  • Crop expansion & diversification: Ongoing avocado acreage expansion (800 acres added since FY2023 with an additional ~200 acres planned through FY2027) and continued wine grape sales growth.
  • Operational water management: Active management of water rights and fallowing agreements in response to Colorado River shortages; California water supply is reported as adequate with improved 2025–26 precipitation expectations.

Original SEC Filing: Limoneira CO [ LMNR ] - 10-Q - Mar. 12, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Adobe CEO to Depart as AI Boosts Sales — Update
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

Adobe said Chief Executive Shantanu Narayen, credited with leading the software company's transformation over the past 18 years, will step down after it finds a new leader for the artificial intelligence era.

Narayen's departure comes as the software company struggles to convince investors that it can outpace AI's industry-wide disruptions. Adobe on Thursday posted higher quarterly sales, but failed to impress Wall Street with its AI-driven revenue.

Shares fell 7% to $250.00 in after-hours trading.

Adobe posted a profit of $1.89 billion, or $4.60 a share, compared with $1.81 billion, or $4.14 a share, a year earlier.

Stripping out certain one-time items, adjusted per-share earnings were $6.06, ahead of the $5.87 anticipated by analysts, according to FactSet.

Revenue rose 12% to $6.40 billion. Analysts surveyed by FactSet forecast revenue of $6.28 billion. Subscription revenue increased 13%.

Adobe's AI-first annualized recurring revenue more than tripled year over year, Narayen said. The company ended the quarter with $26.06 billion in ARR, in line with Wall Street's estimate. ARR is a crucial indicator for investors, who are anxious to see Adobe turn its AI investments into sales.

Narayen, who joined Adobe in 1998, said in a letter to employees that he will remain involved with the company throughout the transition as chair of the board. He plans to work with Adobe's lead independent director, Frank Calderoni, on the search for his successor.

"This is not a goodbye by any means, but a time for reflection," Narayen said.

Concerns that AI products will replace software tools this week weighed on stocks of smaller companies including UiPath, while bigger enterprises such as Oracle managed to evade a slide in shares.

"The next era of creativity is being written right now - shaped by AI, by new workflows and by entirely new forms of expression," Narayen said in his letter.

Narayen's strategy has been to use AI to expand Adobe's customer base. The goal: for Adobe's entire book of business to be driven by AI-influenced revenue streams.

Adobe has integrated third-party AI models into existing products such as Photoshop and Premiere Pro, and launched its own AI product, Firefly. In December, the company said it was luring more business from enterprise companies as a result of its AI efforts.

In the current second quarter, Adobe expects revenue will be $6.43 billion to $6.48 billion and adjusted earnings per share to be $5.80 to $5.85. Analysts are projecting 6.43 billion in sales and $5.68 a share in adjusted earnings.

Adobe reaffirmed its full-year guidance.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com

data.title: Volato Group 2025 10-K: Revenue $78.6M, EPS $1.18
data.description:
Volato Group reported fiscal 2025 revenue of $78.6 million and delivered net income of $5.17 million, producing diluted and basic earnings per share of $1.18. Revenue doubled year-over-year driven primarily by aircraft sales, while net income benefited from settlements and gains from discontinued operations. The company also recorded an operating income turnaround versus the prior year as cost reductions and aircraft sales improved results.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $78.6 million, up $39.5 million (101%) versus prior year.
  • Gross Profit: $14.7 million.
  • Operating Income: $3.96 million, improved from an operating loss of $8.62 million in 2024.
  • Net Income: $5.17 million, compared with a net loss of $40.65 million in 2024.
  • Net Income (Loss) Per Share: $1.18 per share (basic and diluted), versus $(24.42) per share in 2024.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Growth Drivers: Revenue growth was driven by increased aircraft sales, including three G280 deliveries, and expanding Vaunt subscription revenue.
  • Channel and Operational Shift: Fleet operations were transitioned to flyExclusive in September 2024; aircraft ownership operations were reclassified as discontinued, reducing ongoing operational burden.
  • Product and Platform Momentum: The Vaunt empty-leg app began producing recurring subscription revenue and development on the platform continued through 2025.
  • Strategic Transactions and Initiatives: Entered a merger agreement to acquire M2i, a critical minerals supply-chain business, and launched an enterprise AI platform focused on Microsoft 365 automation.

Original SEC Filing: Volato Group, Inc. [ SOAR ] - 10-K - Mar. 12, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Oncology Institute 2025 10-K: $502.7M Revenue, $(0.54) EPS
data.description:
Oncology Institute reported total operating revenue of $502.7 million for the year, a 27.8% increase driven primarily by specialty pharmacy and patient services, while net loss narrowed to $60.6 million. The company posted net loss per share of $(0.54) basic and diluted, improved from $(0.71) the prior year. Operating performance showed improvement with a reduced loss from operations of $(36.1) million and positive operating cash flow in the fourth quarter.

Financial Highlights

  • Total operating revenue: $502.7 million (27.8% increase vs 2024)
  • Loss from operations (Operating Income): $(36.1) million (improved from $(60.1) million in 2024)
  • Net loss (Net Income): $(60.6) million (modest improvement vs $(64.7) million in 2024)
  • Net loss per share (Net Income Per Share): $(0.54) per share basic and diluted (improved from $(0.71) in 2024)

Business Highlights

  • Revenue mix and channel shift: Specialty pharmacy became the largest revenue channel, representing 53.5% of revenue with fills up 66.6%; patient services contributed $229.0 million and specialty pharmacy $269.2 million.
  • Network expansion: Expanded to 65 community practices and a 207-provider network across 17 markets, managing roughly 2.0 million lives under value-based contracts.
  • Operational efficiency: Patient services fee‑for‑service volumes increased 9.0% and capitation volumes increased 17.2%; clinical payroll optimized through greater use of advanced practice providers (APPs) and alignment of physician compensation.
  • Strategic partnerships and clinical operations: Clinical trials operations transitioned to an outsourced model with Helios under a revenue-share agreement and supporting transition services to streamline research functions.
  • Cash flow and SG&A trends: Operating cash use for the year was $(24.6) million with positive operating cash flow in Q4 and a reduction in SG&A of approximately 2.1% for the year.

Original SEC Filing: Oncology Institute, Inc. [ TOI ] - 10-K - Mar. 12, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: M3-Brigade Acquisition V 10-K: Net Income $5.78M, EPS $0.16
data.description:
M3-Brigade Acquisition V reported net income of $5.78 million for the year ended December 31, 2025, driven primarily by $12.26 million of interest income on its Trust Account offset by $6.48 million of operating losses. The company recorded operating loss (loss from operations) of $6.48 million and reported basic and diluted net income per share of $0.16 for both Class A and Class B shares. The filing reflects activity typical of a blank check company preparing for a business combination and transition to a public operating entity.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Income: $5.78 million (primarily reflecting $12.26 million interest on Trust Account less $6.48 million operating costs)
  • Operating Income (Loss): $(6.48) million (Loss from operations / general and administrative costs)
  • Net Income Per Share: $0.16 per Class A share and $0.16 per Class B share, basic and diluted for year ended Dec 31, 2025

Business Highlights

  • Business model: Cayman-incorporated blank check company formed to complete a business combination.
  • Capital deployment: IPO proceeds of $288.9 million placed in a Trust Account to finance a target acquisition and post-merger operations.
  • Transaction milestone: Entered a Business Combination Agreement to domesticate to Delaware and merge to form a publicly traded Pubco.
  • Operational activity: Primary activities to date include target identification, due diligence, and preparatory actions for public-company compliance.
  • Liquidity & outlook: Ongoing use of trust interest and sponsor loans to support deal execution; closing of the transaction expected in Q2 2026, subject to customary conditions.

Original SEC Filing: M3-Brigade Acquisition V Corp. [ MBAV ] - 10-K - Mar. 12, 2026

data.title: Legacy Housing 10-K: Revenue $164.6M, EPS $1.74
data.description:
Legacy Housing reported fiscal year revenue of $164.6 million and diluted earnings per share of $1.74, reflecting a decline in sales and profitability versus the prior year. The company cited lower product and land sales volumes and higher selling, general and administrative expenses as primary drivers of the year-over-year decrease in results. Net income for the year was $41.8 million with an income tax expense of $9.8 million.

Financial Highlights

  • Total net revenue: $164.6 million, down $19.6 million (−10.7%) versus prior year.
  • Income from operations: $48.4 million, down $15.2 million (−23.9%) versus prior year.
  • Net income: $41.8 million, down $19.8 million (−32.2%) versus prior year.
  • Net income per share (Basic and Diluted): $1.74 (both Basic and Diluted), down from Basic $2.55 / Diluted $2.48 in prior year.
  • Income tax expense: $9.8 million.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue & volume trends: Product sales fell approximately 9.6% year-over-year as units sold declined about 20%, while net revenue per unit increased roughly 13% due to price increases.
  • Channel mix shift: Retail store sales grew about 12.7% and direct dealer cash sales increased, offsetting a 30% decline in commercial manufactured home park (MHP) sales.
  • Brand & distribution: Continued multi-channel distribution through the Legacy brand via more than 80 independent retailers, 14 company stores and direct MHP sales.
  • Operational capacity & expansion: Operates three U.S. factories with capacity to produce roughly 60 homes per week; the Georgia facility can add capacity with additional investment.
  • Strategic land & development: Acquired multiple land parcels for community and subdivision development and offers financing tied to community site builds.

Original SEC Filing: Legacy Housing Corp [ LEGH ] - 10-K - Mar. 12, 2026

data.title: CuriosityStream 10-K: $71.7M Revenue, $(0.11) Net Loss per Share
data.description:
CuriosityStream reported fiscal year revenues of $71.7 million and a net loss per share of $(0.11), reflecting year-over-year revenue growth and improved profitability metrics; management attributes top-line expansion to content licensing. The company recorded an operating loss of $(7.3) million and a net loss of $(6.4) million, both improved from the prior year, signaling operational progress alongside revenue gains.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenues: $71.7 million (40% increase vs prior year; prior year $51.1 million)
  • Operating loss: $(7.3) million (improvement from prior year operating loss of $(13.3) million)
  • Net loss: $(6.4) million (improved from $(12.9) million prior year)
  • Net loss per share (Basic and Diluted): $(0.11) (improved from $(0.24) prior year)
  • Gross Profit: $40.5 million (reported as implied in summaries)

Business Highlights

  • Management discussion and outlook: Management focused on operational performance and provided an outlook in Part II, Item 7 emphasizing key drivers for continued improvement.
  • Revenue drivers: Growth was driven primarily by expansion in content licensing.
  • Channel shifts: Noted movement across sales channels with emphasis on expanding digital and customer-direct distribution and adjustments in other distribution partnerships.
  • Brand and marketing: Increased marketing and product initiatives aimed at boosting brand awareness and improving customer retention.
  • Operational milestones: Management highlighted supply chain optimizations, capacity adjustments, and investments in scalability to support future growth.

Original SEC Filing: CuriosityStream Inc. [ CURI ] - 10-K - Mar. 12, 2026

data.title: BuzzFeed Issues Going Concern Warning, Lacks Liquidity For Coming Year
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

BuzzFeed warned that it lacks the resources to fund its cash obligations during the next year without a plan to address its capital needs.

The digital media company said it has broadly operated at a loss since its conception and is burdened by legacy commitments, Chief Financial Officer Matt Omer said Thursday. The company has engaged in strategic conversations to unlock the value of its assets and address liquidity issues, and is exploring strategic options to position itself for profitability, he said.

"We believe there is a gap between the value of our individual assets and our market capitalization that suggests significant unrecognized upside," Chief Executive Jonah Peretti said.

Shares tumbled 16% to 60 cents a share in post-market trading following the statement. The company received a non-compliance warning from Nasdaq that it failed to meet the exchange's minimum bid requirement earlier this month.

BuzzFeed ended 2025 with unrestricted cash and cash equivalents of $8.5 million and an accumulated deficit of $679.6 million.

BuzzFeed previously issued a warning about its ability to continue as a going concern in May, and took out a $40 million loan to fully repay its obligations to certain debtholders. That concern was resolved by September, the company said in a filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission.

The company borrowed an incremental $5 million following the loan, and said Thursday it pushed back a $5 million payment initially due in February to the end of April.

BuzzFeed reported a fourth-quarter loss of $26.8 million, or 72 cents a share. A year earlier, the company had reported a profit of $31 million, including $35.2 million in income from discontinued operations, and logged an 11 cent loss per share.

Revenue was $56.5 million, compared with $56.2 million a year earlier.

The company said it is withholding its full-year guidance is it evaluates strategic opportunities.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: Bimini Capital Q4 net income rises to $3.4 mln, says war in Iran and inflation impacts forecast
data.description:
Overview

  • US mortgage securities investor reported Q4 net income of $3.4 mln and EPS of $0.34

  • Advisory service revenue rose to $4.7 mln, driven by higher Orchid Island equity

  • Company approved $2.5 mln share repurchase plan over next 24 months

Outlook

  • Company expects TJIM acquisition to close at the beginning of Q2 2026

  • Bimini says economic outlook has become very uncertain due to conflict in Iran and potential higher inflation

  • Company plans to operate a smaller Agency RMBS portfolio with lower leverage after TJIM acquisition

Result Drivers

  • FAVORABLE FIXED INCOME MARKETS - Co said calm fixed income markets and declining interest rate volatility supported Agency RMBS and other fixed income sectors in Q4

  • HIGHER ADVISORY REVENUE - Advisory service revenue rose to $4.7 mln, mainly due to increased equity at Orchid Island Capital, which Bimini manages

  • INVESTMENT GAINS - Co recorded mark-to-market and unrealized gains on MBS portfolio and investment in Orchid during Q4

data.title: Qnity Drops 7.9%. It Was a Bad Day for Semiconductor Stocks. — Barrons.com
data.description:
Al Root

Semiconductor stocks took it on the chin early Thursday. The main reasons are likely unrelated to Nvidia, AI, or hyperscalers' spending. Instead, investors should look at oil and interest rates.

Shares of semiconductor manufacturing materials company Qnity Electronics, which was spun out from DuPont de Nemours in late 2025, were down 7.4% in midday trading. They were, at times, the worst-performing shares in the S&P 500.

They weren't alone. Shares of peer Entegris were down 4.9%. ASML stock was off 2.1%. Intel shares were down 4.1%.

There isn't any semi-specific news to explain the declines. There were no material updates from Amazon or Meta Platforms and no significant upgrades or downgrades from Wall Street.

Instead, interest rates are up, with investors fearing a bout of oil price-induced inflation.

The yield on the 10-year Treasury was at 4.244%, up on the day, and up roughly 0.3 percentage points since the start of fighting in Iran. The price of Brent crude, the international benchmark, was up 8% to near $100 a barrel.

Semiconductor manufacturing is a capital-intensive, long-cycle, cyclical business. It takes years to build chip-making fabs, and those fabs need to operate at full capacity to generate big profits.

The semi industry can be sensitive to interest rates because higher rates, along with their impact on economic growth, make investments more costly, slowing investment spending, explains Adrian Helfert, CIO of Multi Asset Strategies at Westwood.

To be sure, any capital-intensive, cyclical industry faces the same headwinds from higher rates. Mining is capital-intensive. Freeport McMoRan stock is off 3.3%, with copper down 0.6%. Caterpillar shares are down 1%, while the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average were both off about 1%.

The semiconductor value chain was hit harder than mining on Thursday. Still, the common reason for the decline appears to be rates.

Write to Al Root at allen.root@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Champions Oncology 10-Q: Q (Oct–Jan) Oncology revenue $16.6M, EPS $(0.02)
data.description:
Champions Oncology reported oncology revenue of $16.6 million for the quarter ended Jan 31, 2026, a 2.8% year-over-year decline, and $45.6 million for the nine months, up 2.2% year-to-date. The company posted a quarterly net loss of approximately $0.3 million (net loss per basic and diluted share of $0.02) and a nine-month net loss of about $0.5 million (net loss per share $0.04). Operating results were pressured versus prior year, with operating loss of roughly $0.3 million for the quarter and $0.6 million year-to-date.

Financial Highlights

  • Oncology revenue (quarter ended Jan 31, 2026): $16.6 million, down 2.8% year-over-year.
  • Oncology revenue (nine months): $45.6 million, up 2.2% year-over-year.
  • Gross profit (quarter): $7.7 million (reported as oncology revenue $16.559M less cost of oncology revenue $8.834M; gross margin approximately 46.7%).
  • Income (loss) from operations: $(0.3) million for the quarter, versus $4.5 million in the prior-year quarter; $(0.6) million for the nine months, versus $6.6 million prior-year.
  • Net income (loss): $(0.3) million for the quarter (approximately $(279)k); $(0.5) million for the nine months (approximately $(508)k).
  • Net income (loss) per common share — basic: $(0.02) for the quarter; $(0.04) for the nine months.
  • Net income (loss) per common share — diluted: $(0.02) for the quarter; $(0.04) for the nine months.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue mix and drivers: Year-to-date oncology revenue growth of 2.2% was driven by pharmacology services; data license revenue experienced lumpy spikes, with TOS data license revenue declining sharply compared with the prior year due to fewer large one-time licenses.
  • Commercial initiatives: The company increased sales and marketing headcount and spending to expand its commercial organization and data licensing efforts.
  • Operational efficiency actions: Higher outsourced lab and radiolabeling costs impacted margins this quarter; management plans to insource work to improve margins going forward.
  • R&D and platform development: Research and development spending rose materially to support sequencing, platform and data development, and validation of discovery targets.
  • Outlook-related actions: Continued investment in commercial capabilities and moves to insource laboratory work are intended to stabilize margin pressure and support future data licensing growth.

Original SEC Filing: CHAMPIONS ONCOLOGY, INC. [ CSBR ] - 10-Q - Mar. 12, 2026
